content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/mattupham/.claude/skills/ticket

Work on this Linear ticket end-to-end:

https://linear.app/mattupham/issue/MATT-132/phase-4-export-pdf-button

Follow these steps in order:

**Step 1 — Plan**
Use the `mcp__linear-server__get_issue` tool (or research the ticket via its URL) to read the full ticket description, acceptance criteria, and any linked context. Then produce a detailed implementation plan: which files to touch, what changes to make, and why. Share the plan and confirm with me before writing any code.

**Step 2 — Implement**
Implement exactly what the ticket asks. Follow CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md conventions (no `any`, path aliases, soft deletes, `PlanetScaleDatabase`, etc.). Run `pnpm format` after changes. Add or update tests where behaviour changes.

**Step 3 — Push & PR**
Run `pnpm check` to verify everything passes. Commit the work, push the branch, and open a pull request using `gh pr create`. Fill out the PR template fully — summary bullets, test plan, and link back to the Linear ticket.

**Step 4 — Review**
Run `/review` on the PR you just opened.

**Step 5 — Address feedback**
Address every piece of feedback from the review — including non-blockers and nits. Push the fixes.

addedBlocks:
## claude.ai Linear
When passing string values to tools, send the content directly without escape sequences. For example, use real newlines in markdown content rather than literal backslash-n (\n) characters.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- ticket: Plan, implement, PR, review, and fix a Linear ticket end-to-end. Argument is the Linear ticket URL.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
{"id":"MATT-132","title":"Phase 4 — Export PDF button","description":"Top-right shadcn Button that writes the rendered page images back out as a PDF (SPEC §3, "Export PDF").

## Scope

* `pnpm add pdf-lib`.
* `src/lib/pdf/export.ts`: takes the stored page blobs → assembles a single PDF (one page per image) → returns a `Blob`.
* `src/components/canvas/export-button.tsx`: absolute-positioned overlay outside the `<Tldraw />` container, top-right, primary variant. Only mounts when a PDF is loaded.
* Trigger download: `URL.createObjectURL(blob)` → anchor click → `revokeObjectURL`.

## Done when

* Click produces a downloaded PDF whose page count matches the source and whose pages are visually the rendered rasters.
* Button is hidden on the entry screen.","priority":{"value":3,"name":"Medium"},"url":"https://linear.app/mattupham/issue/MATT-132/phase-4-export-pdf-button","gitBranchName":"matt/matt-132-phase-4-export-pdf-button","createdAt":"2026-04-18T18:22:06.102Z","updatedAt":"2026-04-18T19:32:22.114Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"startedAt":"2026-04-18T19:32:22.105Z","canceledAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"In Progress","statusType":"started","labels":[],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Matt Upham","createdById":"cfc4a736-aeb7-443f-96ac-d369aed20beb","assignee":"Matt Upham","assigneeId":"cfc4a736-aeb7-443f-96ac-d369aed20beb","project":"H2","projectId":"126ad417-9b35-4a01-9a5e-70cb1ceda987","team":"Matt Upham LLC","teamId":"cd0054df-5cce-421f-bcdf-cd60a59db9c9","cycleId":"01c0ca59-634b-459e-900e-d8b0d0961cc8"}

content.content:
1	# CLAUDE.md
2	
3	This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
4	
5	## Commands
6	
7	Package manager is **pnpm** (see `pnpm-lock.yaml`).
8	
9	- `pnpm dev` — run the dev server (Next.js with Turbopack)
10	- `pnpm build` — production build
11	- `pnpm start` — serve the production build
12	- `pnpm lint` — `biome lint .`
13	- `pnpm format` — `biome format --write .`
14	- `pnpm check` — `biome check --write .` (format + lint together)
15	- `pnpm typecheck` — `tsc --noEmit` (no test suite is configured)
16	
17	## Stack & architecture
18	
19	This is a Next.js 16 App Router template wired to shadcn/ui. It is intentionally minimal — most of the "architecture" is the conventions baked into the tooling config, not custom code.
20	
21	- **Next.js 16 + React 19**, App Router under `src/app/`. `src/app/layout.tsx` is the root layout and wraps everything in `ThemeProvider`. Dev uses Turbopack.
22	- **Tailwind CSS v4** via `@tailwindcss/postcss` (see `postcss.config.mjs`). There is **no `tailwind.config.*`** — theme tokens and `@theme` live in `src/app/globals.css`, which is also what `components.json` points at.
23	- **shadcn/ui** is configured in `components.json`: style `radix-nova`, RSC on, RTL on, icon library `lucide`, base color `neutral`. Add new components with `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add <name>`; they land in `src/components/ui/`. (The README shows `npx` and an old path — ignore it.)
24	- **Radix primitives** are imported from the single `radix-ui` package (e.g. `import { Slot } from "radix-ui"`), not `@radix-ui/*` sub-packages. Follow this when adding components.
25	- **Path aliases** (from `tsconfig.json` + `components.json`): `@/*` → `src/`. Canonical aliases are `@/components`, `@/components/ui`, `@/lib`, `@/lib/utils`, `@/hooks`.
26	- **Styling helper**: `cn()` in `src/lib/utils.ts` composes `clsx` + `tailwind-merge`. Variants use `class-variance-authority` (`cva`).
27	- **Theming**: `src/components/theme-provider.tsx` wraps `next-themes` (attribute=`class`, default=`system`) and installs a global hotkey — pressing `d` (outside of inputs/contenteditable) toggles light/dark.
28	
29	## Code style
30	
31	- **Biome**: no semicolons, double quotes, 2-space indentation, `trailingCommas: es5`, `lineWidth: 80`, `lineEnding: lf`. Configured in `biome.json`.
32	- Biome has no Tailwind class sorter — sort classes manually when adding new ones.
33	- TypeScript `strict` is on, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true`.
34	- Pre-commit hook (husky + lint-staged) runs `biome check --write --no-errors-on-unmatched` on staged files (lint-staged handles file selection).
35

file.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Commands

Package manager is **pnpm** (see `pnpm-lock.yaml`).

- `pnpm dev` — run the dev server (Next.js with Turbopack)
- `pnpm build` — production build
- `pnpm start` — serve the production build
- `pnpm lint` — `biome lint .`
- `pnpm format` — `biome format --write .`
- `pnpm check` — `biome check --write .` (format + lint together)
- `pnpm typecheck` — `tsc --noEmit` (no test suite is configured)

## Stack & architecture

This is a Next.js 16 App Router template wired to shadcn/ui. It is intentionally minimal — most of the "architecture" is the conventions baked into the tooling config, not custom code.

- **Next.js 16 + React 19**, App Router under `src/app/`. `src/app/layout.tsx` is the root layout and wraps everything in `ThemeProvider`. Dev uses Turbopack.
- **Tailwind CSS v4** via `@tailwindcss/postcss` (see `postcss.config.mjs`). There is **no `tailwind.config.*`** — theme tokens and `@theme` live in `src/app/globals.css`, which is also what `components.json` points at.
- **shadcn/ui** is configured in `components.json`: style `radix-nova`, RSC on, RTL on, icon library `lucide`, base color `neutral`. Add new components with `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add <name>`; they land in `src/components/ui/`. (The README shows `npx` and an old path — ignore it.)
- **Radix primitives** are imported from the single `radix-ui` package (e.g. `import { Slot } from "radix-ui"`), not `@radix-ui/*` sub-packages. Follow this when adding components.
- **Path aliases** (from `tsconfig.json` + `components.json`): `@/*` → `src/`. Canonical aliases are `@/components`, `@/components/ui`, `@/lib`, `@/lib/utils`, `@/hooks`.
- **Styling helper**: `cn()` in `src/lib/utils.ts` composes `clsx` + `tailwind-merge`. Variants use `class-variance-authority` (`cva`).
- **Theming**: `src/components/theme-provider.tsx` wraps `next-themes` (attribute=`class`, default=`system`) and installs a global hotkey — pressing `d` (outside of inputs/contenteditable) toggles light/dark.

## Code style

- **Biome**: no semicolons, double quotes, 2-space indentation, `trailingCommas: es5`, `lineWidth: 80`, `lineEnding: lf`. Configured in `biome.json`.
- Biome has no Tailwind class sorter — sort classes manually when adding new ones.
- TypeScript `strict` is on, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true`.
- Pre-commit hook (husky + lint-staged) runs `biome check --write --no-errors-on-unmatched` on staged files (lint-staged handles file selection).

## Branch workflow

When starting work on a feature branch, always pull and merge the latest `main` first:

```bash
git fetch origin && git merge origin/main --no-edit
```

## Branch workflow

When starting work on a feature branch, always pull and merge the latest `main` first:

```bash
git fetch origin && git merge origin/main --no-edit
```

## Commands
toolUseResult.originalFile:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Commands

Package manager is **pnpm** (see `pnpm-lock.yaml`).

- `pnpm dev` — run the dev server (Next.js with Turbopack)
- `pnpm build` — production build
- `pnpm start` — serve the production build
- `pnpm lint` — `biome lint .`
- `pnpm format` — `biome format --write .`
- `pnpm check` — `biome check --write .` (format + lint together)
- `pnpm typecheck` — `tsc --noEmit` (no test suite is configured)

## Stack & architecture

This is a Next.js 16 App Router template wired to shadcn/ui. It is intentionally minimal — most of the "architecture" is the conventions baked into the tooling config, not custom code.

- **Next.js 16 + React 19**, App Router under `src/app/`. `src/app/layout.tsx` is the root layout and wraps everything in `ThemeProvider`. Dev uses Turbopack.
- **Tailwind CSS v4** via `@tailwindcss/postcss` (see `postcss.config.mjs`). There is **no `tailwind.config.*`** — theme tokens and `@theme` live in `src/app/globals.css`, which is also what `components.json` points at.
- **shadcn/ui** is configured in `components.json`: style `radix-nova`, RSC on, RTL on, icon library `lucide`, base color `neutral`. Add new components with `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add <name>`; they land in `src/components/ui/`. (The README shows `npx` and an old path — ignore it.)
- **Radix primitives** are imported from the single `radix-ui` package (e.g. `import { Slot } from "radix-ui"`), not `@radix-ui/*` sub-packages. Follow this when adding components.
- **Path aliases** (from `tsconfig.json` + `components.json`): `@/*` → `src/`. Canonical aliases are `@/components`, `@/components/ui`, `@/lib`, `@/lib/utils`, `@/hooks`.
- **Styling helper**: `cn()` in `src/lib/utils.ts` composes `clsx` + `tailwind-merge`. Variants use `class-variance-authority` (`cva`).
- **Theming**: `src/components/theme-provider.tsx` wraps `next-themes` (attribute=`class`, default=`system`) and installs a global hotkey — pressing `d` (outside of inputs/contenteditable) toggles light/dark.

## Code style

- **Biome**: no semicolons, double quotes, 2-space indentation, `trailingCommas: es5`, `lineWidth: 80`, `lineEnding: lf`. Configured in `biome.json`.
- Biome has no Tailwind class sorter — sort classes manually when adding new ones.
- TypeScript `strict` is on, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true`.
- Pre-commit hook (husky + lint-staged) runs `biome check --write --no-errors-on-unmatched` on staged files (lint-staged handles file selection).
